summary: Russia launched a combined overnight attack on Ukraine using 2 Oniks/Caliber cruise missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with impacts at 11 locations. Russian forces claim to have liberated Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region. Russian President Putin held a meeting on fuel market stabilization, revealing gasoline reserves of 1.7 million tons and discussing a potential full ban on diesel exports.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight with 2 Oniks/Caliber cruise missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with impacts at 11 locations and debris at 13 others. (Source: @UkraineNow/82232)
Russian MoD claims liberation of Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)
Putin disclosed Russian gasoline reserves at 1.7 million tons, nearly last year's level, and discussed a potential full ban on diesel fuel exports. (Source: @tass_agency/382796)
Multiple UAVs tracked heading toward Kyiv with course changes noted over Obukhiv and Vasylkiv, with ballistic missile threats reported for the capital. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68142)
Russian forces struck Zaporizhzhia with KAB bombs, killing 2 and injuring 17 including a child. (Source: @eRadarrua/98536)
Ukraine's Finance Minister Marchenko announced Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing in 2026–2029. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224)
Russian fuel queues persist with prices surging 1.5x in Miass, Chelyabinsk region, as Russia begins tapping fuel reserves. (Source: @eRadarrua/98517)
Drones and guided aviation bombs targeted Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Kirovohrad regions throughout the day. (Source: @kpszsu/66662)

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a large combined overnight missile and drone attack on Ukraine employing 142 drones, 6 ballistic missiles, and 2 cruise missiles across 11 impact locations. Russian forces claim territorial gains with reported liberation of Pisantsy and Novoselovka in eastern Ukraine. Domestically, Russia faces fuel supply challenges with Putin convening emergency meetings as gasoline reserves stand at 1.7 million tons and diesel export bans are under discussion.

points: Ukraine's Air Force reported that nearly all ballistic missiles were intercepted during the overnight attack; 5 Iskander-M from Bryansk and 2 Tsirkon from Kursk regions were launched. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68123)

The overnight Russian strike package included 2 Oniks/Caliber anti-ship missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with hits at 11 locations and debris falls at 13 additional locations. (Source: @UkraineNow/82232)
In Zaporizhzhia, Russian KAB-guided bombs struck the regional center, killing 2 people and injuring 17 including a child. (Source: @eRadarrua/98536)

Dnipropetrovsk region was attacked over 40 times during the day with drones and artillery; one man killed, one injured, gas stations hit, a sports school caught fire, and two people injured. (Source: @UkraineNow/82229)

Sumy region experienced guided aviation bomb strikes damaging industrial and warehouse facilities, followed by a strike drone hitting a residential building roof and injuring a woman. (Source: @UkraineNow/82234)
Ukraine's Finance Minister Sergey Marchenko announced at a Gdansk forum that Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing in 2026–2029. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224)

Seven Ukrainian civilians illegally held in Russia were returned home; Ombudsman Lubinets estimates approximately 16,000 Ukrainian civilians may remain in Russian captivity. (Source: @UkraineNow/82230)

President Putin held a meeting on fuel market stabilization, revealing gasoline reserves of 1.7 million tons — nearly at last year's level — and stating Russia has begun using fuel reserves. (Source: @tass_agency/382796)
Putin discussed the possibility of a complete ban on diesel fuel exports and ordered systemic measures to stabilize the domestic fuel market. (Source: @tass_agency/382798)
Fuel queues persist at Russian gas stations with required gasoline grades sometimes unavailable; prices in Miass surged 1.5x within days. (Source: @eRadarrua/98517)
The Russian MoD reported that air defense shot down 72 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Sea of Azov waters in one day. (Source: @tass_agency/382804)
Russian forces claim liberation of Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)
The Slavyansk NPP in Krasnodar Krai, recently restored after previous strikes, was again hit and shown burning in footage. (Source: @eRadarrua/98502)
Putin stated that refinery capacities are being used at maximum and instructed efforts to maintain fuel supply schedules for the agricultural sector. (Source: @tass_agency/382801)
Russian MoD claims forces liberated Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)
Drones from Donetsk region tracked northwest past Novodonetsk, and 3 drones from Dnipropetrovsk region headed toward Yurivka from Droprillya. (Source: @kpszsu/66670)
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aviation bomb strikes on Dnipropetrovsk region and Zaporizhzhia region. (Source: @kpszsu/66662)
Zaporizhzhia regional center was struck with KAB-guided bombs, resulting in 2 dead and 17 injured including a child. (Source: @eRadarrua/98536)
The FOBOS 46th Airmobile Brigade unit released video showing continued operations against enemy personnel in the area. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23600)
Two controlled aviation bombs were reported between Synelnykove and Pavlohrad in Dnipropetrovsk region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98514)
Multiple UAVs tracked from Chernihiv region toward Koriukivka (1), Pornytsia (2), and Novhorod-Siverskyi (2), with 6 drones circling near Kholmiv and Pornytsia. (Source: @eRadarrua/98535)
An intelligence-type UAV was spotted near Horodnia and Semenivka in Chernihiv region. (Source: @eRadarrua/98519)
UAVs from Sumy region tracked west past Buryn and Zinkiv toward Poltava region, with 2 drones heading toward Stepanivka from the north. (Source: @kpszsu/66658)

Sumy city experienced guided aviation bomb strikes damaging industrial and warehouse facilities, followed by a strike drone hitting a residential roof and injuring a woman. (Source: @UkraineNow/82234)
Russian ballistic missile threat reported toward Kharkiv, with drones also tracked in Kharkiv region heading toward Bohodukhiv and Vovchansk/Peresichne. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68156)
UAVs from Mykolaiv region tracked on a northwestern course toward Novyi Buh/Kazanka in Kirovohrad region. (Source: @kpszsu/66671)
One drone targeted Kamianske reservoir approaching Kremenchuk, with another south of Reshetylivka; one jet-type UAV reported over Poltavshchyna near Dykanka. (Source: @eRadarrua/98512)
Drones tracked over Dnipropetrovsk region near Pokrov heading north, and over northern Dnipropetrovsk region near Yurivka heading west. (Source: @kpszsu/66654)
Kirovohrad region drones tracked toward Kropyvnytskyi. (Source: @kpszsu/66673)
Russian forces struck Zaporizhzhia with KAB-guided bombs, killing 2 and injuring 17 including a child. (Source: @eRadarrua/98536)
Traffic on the Crimean Bridge was temporarily closed according to the situation monitoring channel. (Source: @tass_agency/382803)

Russia's overnight combined strike included 2 Oniks/Caliber cruise missiles, 6 Iskander-M/S-400 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones; impacts at 11 locations and debris at 13 others. (Source: @UkraineNow/82232)
Ukrainian Air Force reported nearly all ballistic missiles were intercepted; 5 Iskander-M launched from Bryansk and 2 Tsirkon from Kursk region. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68123)
Russian MoD claimed air defense shot down 72 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Sea of Azov in one day. (Source: @tass_agency/382804)

Multiple ballistic missile threats were issued for Kyiv with course changes tracked over Obukhiv and Vasylkiv; Iskander-M launch threat reported from Taganrog, Rostov region. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68155)
Russian tactical aviation conducted multiple guided aviation bomb strikes on Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, and Sumy regions. (Source: @kpszsu/66644)
A source indicated forthcoming positive news regarding the destruction of several enemy aviation assets. (Source: @eRadarrua/98487)

key_developments: Russia launched a combined overnight attack with 142 drones, 6 ballistic missiles, and 2 cruise missiles striking 11 locations across Ukraine. (Source: @UkraineNow/82232)
Russian forces claim liberation of Pisantsy in Dnepropetrovsk region and Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31221)
Zaporizhzhia city center struck by KAB-guided bombs killing 2 and injuring 17 including a child. (Source: @eRadarrua/98536)
Putin revealed gasoline reserves at 1.7 million tons and discussed potential full ban on diesel exports amid domestic fuel queues. (Source: @tass_agency/382796)
Ukraine announced need for nearly $146 billion in external financing for 2026–2029 following meetings with UK and Lithuanian finance ministers. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224)
Multiple ballistic missile threats targeted Kyiv with course changes over Obukhiv and Vasylkiv; Iskander-M threat from Taganrog. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68155)
Dnipropetrovsk region attacked over 40 times in one day with drones and artillery, one killed, one injured, gas stations and infrastructure hit. (Source: @UkraineNow/82229)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is escalating with Russia deploying large combined strike packages of 142 drones alongside cruise and ballistic missiles in a single night. The Iskander-M launches from Bryansk and Tsirkon from Kursk indicate continued use of western Russian launch domains. Multiple simultaneous ballistic missile threats toward Kyiv with mid-course corrections suggest adaptive targeting. On the ground, Russian claims of liberating Pisantsy and Novoselovka indicate continued grinding operations in the eastern front. Russia's domestic fuel situation is emerging as a vulnerability, with Putin convening emergency meetings and considering export bans.
sections.regional_response: Finnish President Stubb stated that neither sanctions nor Russian military losses will end the war, but growing Russian public demand for peace could compel Putin to stop. US President Biden accused Trump of undermining NATO and damaging American credibility at a Democratic Party convention in Maryland. Polish President Nawrocki's approval rose to 54.8% after Zelensky was stripped of the Order of the White Eagle. UK Prime Minister Starmer announced his decision to resign. The Slavyansk refinery in Krasnodar Krai, recently restored after prior strikes, was hit again, suggesting continued Ukrainian pressure on Russian energy infrastructure.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Russian fuel situation warrants continued monitoring: gasoline reserves at 1.7 million tons mask regional disparities with queues and 1.5x price surges in cities like Miass. The potential diesel export ban signals domestic supply concerns. The 72 Ukrainian drones claimed shot down by Russian AD in one day represents a significant Ukrainian effort depth. The FOBOS 46th Airmobile Brigade's continued counteroperations suggest Ukrainian forces remain active in contested areas. The $146 billion financing gap underscores Ukraine's sustained dependency on Western financial support through 2029.
